endpoint_summary: Returns a list of posts. Each post is an object containing an identifier for the author, a unique post ID, a title, and a body of text. The response is a JSON array of post objects, typically used to retrieve blog-style or forum-style content entries.

field_descriptions.userId: The unique identifier of the user (author) who created the post.
field_descriptions.id: The unique identifier for the post itself.
field_descriptions.title: The title or headline of the post.
field_descriptions.body: The main text content of the post. May contain newline characters indicating paragraph breaks.

use_cases: Fetching a list of blog posts to display on a homepage or feed.
Retrieving all posts authored by a specific user by filtering on the userId field.
Displaying post titles in a summary list view, with the option to expand and show the full body.
Paginating through content entries in a content management system.
Using as mock or placeholder data for front-end prototyping and testing.
example:
GET /posts — Returns an array of post objects. Example item: { "userId": 1, "id": 1, "title": "sunt aut facere repellat provident occaecati excepturi optio reprehenderit", "body": "quia et suscipit
suscipit recusandae consequuntur expedita et cum
reprehenderit molestiae ut ut quas totam
nostrum rerum est autem sunt rem eveniet architecto" }
